rulings.Case Title: JAY ZALOWITZ VS. DAVID SMOOKE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 17, 2025 Line 1. DEFENDANT CROWELL & MORING LLP (NON-PARTY) DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Off calendar for failure to meet and confer in person, by telephone, or by video conference as required by CCP 430.41. The response to the amended complaint is now due by May 17, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO MALDONADO VS. ALTAIS, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 17, 2025 Line 9. DEFENDANTs ALTAIS HEALTH SOLUTIONS, ALTAIS MEDICAL GROUP, INC.,and ALTAIS HEALTH, LLC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Defendants Altais, Altais Health Solutions, Altais Medical Group, Inc. and Altais Health, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendants' alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. GULIA OMENE DMD LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 17, 2025 Line 4. PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc.'s motion for assignment of rights, restraining order and turnover order, for which no opposition was received, is GRANTED. Moving Party is advised going forward the court will not accept or will order off calendar motions not noticed for a single department and/or not noticed for the correct department and assigned bench officer. Moving Party is admonished that the court will order off calendar any motion where the moving party fails to timely submit a coherent proposed order that is consistent with the motion. Moving Party is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on April 17, 2025. Moving Party shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. failure to do so may result in the court ordering the motion off calendar. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
